Name: quantization_sensitivity_analysis
Title: Quantization Sensitivity Analysis: A Training-Free Framework for Adaptive Mixed Precision in Neural Networks
Short Hypothesis: A training-free sensitivity analysis framework can effectively determine optimal mixed precision configurations for neural networks by identifying and preserving critical weights, thus addressing performance degradation in quantized models without extensive retraining.
Related Work: While existing works such as SliM-LLM and EMQ address mixed precision quantization, they often rely on extensive training or calibration data. This proposal distinguishes itself by employing a sensitivity analysis framework that operates without these requirements, focusing on the preservation of critical weights derived from intrinsic weight structures and their contributions to model performance.
Abstract: This proposal introduces a novel training-free framework for adaptive mixed precision quantization of neural networks, leveraging sensitivity analysis to identify and preserve critical weights. Existing mixed precision quantization methods often suffer from performance degradation due to inappropriate bit-width allocations across layers. By conducting a structured sensitivity analysis, we aim to quantify the impact of weight precision on model performance. We hypothesize that certain weights are intrinsically more significant, and by preserving their precision while adjusting the quantization levels of less critical weights, we can achieve a more effective quantization strategy. This approach will be validated through empirical studies on various neural network architectures, including CNNs and transformers, using well-established benchmarks. The expected outcome is a set of guidelines for implementing adaptive mixed precision quantization that maintains high accuracy while optimizing computational efficiency. This work will contribute to advancing training-free quantization techniques, offering a robust solution to the challenges faced in deploying quantized models in resource-constrained environments.
Experiments: 1. Implement a sensitivity analysis on selected neural networks (e.g., ResNet, ViT) to determine the impact of weight precision on performance.
2. Develop an adaptive quantization algorithm that preserves critical weights based on the sensitivity analysis results while varying the bit-width of less important weights.

3. Compare performance metrics (accuracy, computational efficiency) of the proposed method against state-of-the-art mixed precision quantization techniques on standard datasets (e.g., ImageNet, CIFAR-10).
4. Evaluate the robustness of the framework by testing it on different model architectures and varying training conditions.
Risk Factors and Limitations: Potential limitations include the reliance on the assumption that certain weights are more critical, which may not hold for all models or datasets. The sensitivity analysis may also be computationally intensive. Additionally, the generalizability of the framework across diverse architectures needs thorough validation.
